email_subject: How AI Helps You Fall BACK in Love With Your Job
email_body:

India leads the world in AI adoption - and in loving their jobs. That’s awkward for anyone still blaming burnout on the system. What if the real bent keys aren’t your manager or your workload… but how you’re using AI? 🗝️ The Work Love Lever Edition 🗝️ By Andrew Allen and Carla Dewing ⚡ AI-Forward People Don’t Hate Their Jobs… Do you love your job… or just tolerate it? Most people think that loving your job is about culture, perks, or a great boss - but the data says otherwise. Globally, only 19% of workers are fully engaged. More than half live paycheck to paycheck. Nearly 75% never get promoted. That’s not tragic luck folks. That’s bent keys everywhere. Now look at India. They lead the world in positive worker sentiment (77%). They also lead in global AI adoption. And 89% of Indian employees say AI has boosted their productivity (vs 62% globally). The country using AI the most… also loves their work. Coincidence? Doubtful. AI-forward pros don’t wait for better managers or lighter workloads. They fix the three forces that make work worth loving. Is This You? If your job feels hollow, stagnant, or capped… maybe it’s not the role. Maybe it’s how you’re using AI. ☑️ Delete the admin sludge: Stop spending your best hours formatting, summarizing, drafting and sorting. Use AI to clear boring work so you can focus on decisions, creativity and visible impact. ☑️ Accelerate your skill curve: Use AI as your tutor, sparring partner and prototype engine. Break down complex ideas. Improve your writing. Stress-test your thinking. Grow faster than your peers. ☑️ Turn output into leverage: When you create better results, faster, you increase your value. That improves your negotiating power, your promotion potential and your earning ceiling. AI doesn’t replace meaning, capability or momentum. It restores them. ✅ Do This Now Identify one repetitive task you hate. Automate it this week. Use AI to level up one skill you’ve been avoiding. Ask yourself: if my results doubled, would my pay increase? If not, WHY. India doesn't love their jobs because AI is trendy. They love their jobs because AI gives them leverage over their time, skills and money. The real question: are you using it well enough to love your work again? 👉 Read Why AI-Forward People Love Their Jobs (Do You?) 📰 Breaking News ⚡ A Quick Update on Remote Work & AI Boomers are clocking out - and taking RTO with them. WFH is staging its comeback tour, healthcare is carrying the entire US job market, and robots are stretching before their first shift. The future of work isn’t broken, it’s just been waiting on new management. WSJ: Fake remote tech workers are funneling paychecks to North Korea, and suddenly global talent needs a background check that includes missile detection. Fortune: As Boomer and Gen X bosses retire, working from home WILL stage a major comeback says this new research. Forbes: Healthcare quietly carried 95% of new US jobs in January (the real future of work is stackable credentials, summer hospital shifts, and skipping the 4-year detour) FII Institute: The future isn’t just AI in the cloud - it’s AI with legs, arms, and a 200-billion-dollar hobby of taking over boring human jobs. Microsoft: AI is spreading - but unless we drop $50 billion to drag the Global South into the AI party, the future of work will come with a ‘for some people only’ stamp. Press play for proof that grit pays in USD… 🛠️ Tool Share ⚡ Stories, Advice & Experiences from Remote AI Pros Inside the Hardest Hiring Process I Ever Passed (+Failed). Alex passed the tests, got the offer, then failed onboarding. Here’s a personal account of what elite hiring really tests and why fit always beats ego. 3 AI Engineering and Technical Management Skills for Leaders. Most execs skipped the part where you lead AI. Catch up before your agents revolt, or your CFO cries. ChatGPT Cheating Is Rising. Here’s How AI-Schools Respond. AI didn’t break school - it just made faking it too easy. Smart schools make cheating more work than learning. 💻 Top Jobs ⚡ Get Hired on Crossover 🌏 Global Remote Roles: SVP of Technical Product Management, 2 Hour Learning - $400,000 USD/year Technical Team Lead, LearnWith.AI - $200,000 USD/year Senior AI Innovation Specialist – Sales, IgniteTech - $100,000 USD/year 🗽 US-Based EdTech Roles: Associate Dean of Parents, Alpha - $200,000 USD/year Elite Guide, gt.school - $150,000 USD/year Reading Specialist, NextGen Academy - $100,000 USD/year 🙋 Ask Crossover ⚡ Expert Voices We asked: “What part of high-performance culture do you secretly love - even though most people would find it intimidating?” "Secretly love (but also hate some days!) feeling like the dumbest person in the room. In a high-performance culture, you won't always be the smartest person in the room, and that can be quite humbling for high achievers! But it also means I'm challenged to think and work at a higher level and that "coasting" doesn't cut it." -          Heather Lother "Secretly love (also hate, as Heather), not having quiet days. High standards don't stop, and high performance is expected daily. If, for some reason, my workload is lower for one or two days, I feel strange 😅." -        Daniela Guarin Monsalve "Surrounded by people who are hard to impress. You don’t get applause for effort. You get respect for outcomes. When someone says “that’s solid,” you know it actually is. Validation means more because it’s not automatic." -          Chintan Parekh 💡 Have a question for a Crossover top performer? Drop it here! 😂 Punchline ⚡ A Laugh Before You Go Andrew Allen is the VP of Content Marketing at Crossover, based in Auckland, New Zealand. He’s worked remotely since 2021, using humor and storytelling to make complex ideas stick. He hosts the world’s most-watched remote work vlog (Out of Office), co-creates one of LinkedIn’s top newsletters (Radically Remote), and contributes to Forbes. Outside work, he’s visited 100+ countries, seen all seven wonders - and his dogs occasionally steal the spotlight. Radically Remote is LinkedIn’s largest community for AI-first remote leaders who want to stay on the pulse but don't have time for a snoozefest. Every 2 weeks we bring you the latest global news, jobs, trends & insights on the future of work. Subscribe to become a higher performing, AI-forward remote leader 👇
